title: extend backwards-compatible staleness bump
purpose: D5.2 defines extend backwards compatible staleness bump for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D5.2` only; siblings under `D5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: When a platform queue item targets extend, a worker improves an existing catalog artifact with backwards-compatible changes—optional script flags, appended playbook sections, extended skills—without breaking callers.
The item must cite parent catalog id; the verifier runs tests and validate-workflow to prove compatibility before dequeue.
On land, [E5.1](E5.1-staleness-design-graph-staleness-json.md) bumps staleness on dependent design nodes and task cards per Plane E reconcile rules.

Extend is preferred over fork when [B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md) compose-first can absorb the change; non-compatible behavior requires fork even if the filename stays the same.
If compatibility proof fails or dependents remain stale without reconcile routing, pursuit stops at H2—never mark extend done while stale design artifacts block implement.

narrative: D5.2 defines extend backwards compatible staleness bump for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system when a platform queue item targets extend, a worker improves an existing catalog artifact with backwards-compatible changes—optional script flags, appended playbook sections, extended skills—without breaking callers, then the item must cite parent catalog id; the verifier runs tests and validate-workflow to prove compatibility before dequeue.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

Extend means backwards-compatible improvement to an existing catalog artifact—add optional flag to script, append playbook section, extend skill without breaking callers. Extension triggers staleness bump on dependent design nodes and task cards per Plane E reconcile rules.

Platform queue items targeting extend must cite parent catalog id and compatibility proof—tests green, validate-workflow passes. Extend is preferred over fork when compose-first can absorb the change ([B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md)). Non-compatible behavior change is fork, not extend, even if filename stays the same—semver and provenance matter.
